summary: Release-aware commands must preserve tranche semantics, and published build/MCP metadata must use the ufo-goat binary names from the provenance manifest.
reason: ufo-goat exposes PURSUE release tranches as user-facing release numbers, while release_batch=0 is reserved for files without a derivable tranche; filtering after the FTS limit or writing during dry-run can produce misleading command results. The published package also ships cmd/ufo-goat-pp-* binaries, so manifest, GoReleaser, Makefile, and MCP docs must not point at stale ufo-pp-* names.
